concepts.title: Methods taken to solve Exponential and Logarithmic Limits when the limit has 0/0 indeterminate form
concepts.conceptBody:
Problems containing exponential and logarithmic functions

Working Rule: 

Using the following formulae whichever is required

(i) $\underset{\text{f}\left( \text{x} \right)\to 0}{\mathop{\mathbf{Lt}}}\,\frac{{{\text{a}}^{\text{f}\left( \text{x} \right)}}-1}{\text{f}\left( \text{x} \right)}=\text{lo}{{\text{g}}_{\text{e}}}\text{a}$

This formula should be used only when base is a constant and power is a variable.

Special Case: 

$\text{L}{{\text{t}}_{f\left( x \right)\to 0}}\frac{{{e}^{f\left( x \right)}}-1}{f\left( x \right)}=1$

(ii) $\underset{\text{f}\left( \text{x} \right)\to 0}{\mathop{\mathbf{Lt}}}\,{{[1+\text{kf}\left( \text{x} \right)]}^{\frac{1}{\text{f}\left( \text{x} \right)}}}={{\text{e}}^{\text{k}}}$

This formula should be used when both base and powers are variables.

(iii) This formula should be used in case of logarithmic function.

(iv) $\underset{\text{f}\left( \text{x} \right)\to 0}{\mathop{\text{Lt}}}\,\frac{\text{log}\left\{ 1+\text{f}\left( \text{x} \right) \right\}}{\text{f}\left( \text{x} \right)}=1$

$=\underset{{}}{\mathop{\underset{\text{x}\to \text{a}}{\mathop{\text{Lt}}}\,{{\text{e}}^{\text{g}\left( \text{x} \right)\left[ \text{f}\left( \text{x} \right)-1 \right]}}}}\,$

Here $\text{x}\to \text{a},\text{f}\left( \text{x} \right)\to 1$ and $\text{g}\left( \text{x} \right)\to \infty $

This formula should be used only when indeterminate form is ${{1}^{\infty }}$.

concepts.title: Problems on Logarithmic and Exponential Limits based on formula
concepts.conceptBody:
Solved Examples

(a) $\underset{x\to 1}{\mathop{Lt}}\,\left( \frac{logx}{x-1} \right)$ 
(b) $\underset{x\to 0}{\mathop{Lt}}\,\frac{{{e}^{x}}-1-x}{{{x}^{2}}}$ 
(c) $\underset{h\to 0}{\mathop{Lt}}\,\left( \frac{log\left( x+h \right)-logx}{h} \right)$ 

Solution: 

(a) given limit is $\text{L}{{\text{t}}_{x\to 1}}\left( \frac{\text{log}x}{x-1} \right)$

replacing $\text{x}$ by $\text{h}+1$, {limit also changes $\}$

$\begin{array}{*{35}{l}} {} & ~=\text{L}{{\text{t}}_{h\to 0}}\frac{\text{log}\left( 1+h \right)}{1+h-1} \\ {} & ~=\text{L}{{\text{t}}_{h\to 0}}\frac{\text{log}\left( 1+h \right)}{h}=1 \\ \end{array}$

(b) $\text{L}{{\text{t}}_{\text{x}\to 0}}\frac{{{\text{e}}^{\text{x}}}-1-\text{x}}{{{\text{x}}^{2}}}$

in this question if we try to use the formula $\left( \frac{{{\text{e}}^{\text{x}}}-1}{\text{x}} \right)$, then it will not be solved, why?

Because we will get zero in numerator & denominator, which becomes unsolvable

$\text{L}{{\text{t}}_{x\to 0}}\frac{\frac{{{e}^{x}}-1}{x}-\frac{1}{x}}{x}=\text{L}{{\text{t}}_{x\to 0}}\frac{\left( \to 1 \right)-1}{x}=\frac{0}{0}\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ form }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }$

so either we use L' hospitals or we go for expansion series.

Here we will go for expansion series

$~=\text{L}{{\text{t}}_{x\to 0}}\frac{\left( 1+\frac{x}{1}+\frac{{{x}^{2}}}{2!}+\frac{{{x}^{3}}}{3!}\ldots \right)-1-x}{{{x}^{2}}}$

$~=\text{L}{{\text{t}}_{x\to 0}}\left( \frac{{{x}^{2}}}{2!}+\frac{{{x}^{2}}}{3!}+\ldots \right)=\text{L}{{\text{t}}_{x\to 0}}\left( \frac{1}{2}+\frac{x}{3!}+\frac{{{x}^{2}}}{4!}\ldots \right)$

putting $\text{x}=0$ in the rest $=\frac{1}{2}$

(c) Given limit is,

This is a simple limit, just use log properties i.e. $\text{log}a-\text{log}b=\text{log}\frac{a}{b}$

$=\text{L}{{\text{t}}_{h\to 0}}\frac{\text{log}\left( \frac{x+h}{x} \right)}{h}$

$=\text{L}{{\text{t}}_{h\to 0}}\frac{\text{log}\left( \frac{1+h}{x} \right)}{h}=\text{L}{{\text{t}}_{h\to 0}}\frac{\text{log}\left( \frac{1+h}{x} \right)}{\frac{h}{x}\times x}$

$=\left( \frac{1}{\text{x}} \right)\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }\left\{ \because \underset{\text{h}\to 0}{\mathop{\text{Lt}}}\,\text{log}\frac{\left( 1+\text{h} \right)}{\text{h}}=1 \right\}$

Another example

(a) Evaluate $li{{m}_{x\to 0}}\frac{{{(ab)}^{x}}-{{a}^{x}}-{{b}^{x}}+1}{{{x}^{2}}}$ 

(b) Evaluate $li{{m}_{x\to 0}}\frac{{{e}^{ta{{n}^{x}}}}-{{e}^{x}}}{tanx-x}$ 

(c) $li{{m}_{x\to 0}}\frac{{{3}^{x}}-{{5}^{x}}}{x}$

(d) $li{{m}_{x\to 0}}\frac{{{6}^{x}}-{{2}^{x}}-{{3}^{x}}+1}{si{{n}^{2}}x}$ 

Solution: 

(a) $\text{li}{{\text{m}}_{x\to 0}}\frac{{{(ab)}^{x}}-{{a}^{x}}-{{b}^{x}}+1}{{{x}^{2}}}$

$=\text{li}{{\text{m}}_{x\to 0}}\frac{{{a}^{x}}{{b}^{x}}-{{a}^{x}}-{{b}^{x}}+1}{{{x}^{2}}}$

$=\text{li}{{\text{m}}_{x\to 0}}=\frac{{{a}^{x}}\left( {{b}^{x}}-1 \right)-\left( {{b}^{x}}-1 \right)}{{{x}^{2}}}$

$=\text{li}{{\text{m}}_{x\to 0}}=\frac{\left( {{a}^{x}}-1 \right)\times \left( {{b}^{x}}-1 \right)}{x}=\text{li}{{\text{m}}_{x\to 0}}\frac{{{a}^{x}}-1}{x}\times \text{li}{{\text{m}}_{x\to 0}}\frac{\left( {{b}^{x}}-1 \right)}{x}=\text{log}a\text{ log}b$

(b) $\text{li}{{\text{m}}_{x\to 0}}\frac{{{e}^{\text{tan}x}}-{{e}^{x}}}{\text{tan}x-x}=\text{li}{{\text{m}}_{x\to 0}}\frac{{{e}^{x}}\times \left[ {{e}^{\left( \text{tan}x-x \right)}}-1 \right]}{\left( \text{tan}x-x \right)}$

$\begin{array}{*{35}{l}} {} & =\underset{x\to 0}{\mathop{\text{lim}}}\,\frac{{{e}^{x}}\left\{ {{e}^{\text{tan}x-x}}-1 \right\}}{\left( \text{tan}x-x \right)}={{e}^{0}}1\left[ \text{as }x\to 0,\text{tan}x-x\to 0 \right] \\ {} & =11=1 \\ \end{array}$

(c) $\text{li}{{\text{m}}_{x\to 0}}\frac{{{3}^{x}}-{{5}^{x}}}{x}=\text{li}{{\text{m}}_{x\to 0}}\left[ \frac{{{3}^{x}}-1}{x}-\frac{{{5}^{x}}-1}{x} \right]$

$=\text{log}3-\text{log}5=\text{log}\frac{3}{5}$

(d) $\text{li}{{\text{m}}_{x\to 0}}\frac{{{6}^{x}}-{{2}^{x}}-{{3}^{x}}+1}{\text{si}{{\text{n}}^{2}}x}$

$=\underset{x\to 0}{\mathop{\text{lim}}}\,\frac{\left. \left( {{2}^{x}}-1 \right){{3}^{x}}-1 \right)}{{{x}^{2}}}\frac{{{x}^{2}}}{\text{si}{{\text{n}}^{2}}x}=\underset{x\to 0}{\mathop{\text{lim}}}\,\frac{{{2}^{x}}-1}{x}\underset{x\to 0}{\mathop{\text{lim}}}\,\frac{{{3}^{x}}-1}{x}\underset{x\to 0}{\mathop{\text{lim}}}\,{{\left( \frac{x}{\text{sin}x} \right)}^{2}}$

$=\text{lo}{{\text{g}}_{e}}3\text{lo}{{\text{g}}_{e}}2$

concepts.title: Methods to solve Logarithmic and Exponential Limits assuming some indeterminate forms other than 0/0 form
concepts.conceptBody:
Problems involving exponential & logarithmic functions

  1. If power is variable, express the given expression as power of e. Use the formula, ${{\text{a}}^{\text{x}}}={{\text{e}}^{\text{xloga}}}$
  2. Use expansion series where required
  3. Use the formula $\underset{\text{f}\left( \text{x} \right)\to 0}{\mathop{\text{Lt}}}\,\left\{ 1+\text{kf}\left( \text{x} \right) \right\}\frac{1}{\text{f}\left( \text{x} \right)}={{\text{e}}^{\text{k}}}$ where $\text{k}$ is constant

Find the limit 

(a) $L{{t}_{x\to \infty }}{{\left( 1+\frac{a}{x} \right)}^{x}}$ 
(b) $\underset{x\to \infty }{\mathop{Lt}}\,x\left( {{e}^{\frac{1}{x}}}-{{e}^{\frac{-1}{x}}} \right)$ 

Solution: 

We try to convert these questions to type 2 only so that we can use formulas.

(a) Given, $\underset{\text{x}\to \infty }{\mathop{\text{Lt}}}\,{{\left( 1+\frac{\text{a}}{\text{x}} \right)}^{\text{x}}}$

Now here it is a type II question only. Compare it with formula $\underset{\text{x}\to 0}{\mathop{\text{Lt}}}\,\left( 1+\text{ax} \right)\frac{1}{\text{x}}$.

if you put $\text{x}=\frac{1}{\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ h}}$, limit changes to

$~=\text{L}{{\text{t}}_{h\to 0}}\left( 1+ah \right)\frac{1}{h}$

$={{e}^{a}}\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }\!\!\{\!\!\text{ using }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }\underset{x\to 0}{\mathop{\text{Lt}}}\,{{(1+x)}^{\frac{1}{x}}}=e$}

(b) $\text{L}{{\text{t}}_{x\to 0}}x\left( {{x}^{\frac{1}{x}}}-{{e}^{-\frac{1}{x}}} \right)$

again putting $\text{x}=\text{x}=\frac{1}{\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ h}}$

$\text{L}{{\text{t}}_{\text{h}\to 0}}\frac{1}{\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ h}}\left( {{\text{e}}^{\text{h}}}-{{\text{e}}^{-\text{h}}} \right)$

$=\text{L}{{\text{t}}_{h\to 0}}\left[ \frac{{{\text{e}}^{\text{h}}}-1}{\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ h}}-\frac{\left( {{\text{e}}^{-\text{h}}}-1 \right)}{\text{h}} \right]=\text{L}{{\text{t}}_{\text{L}\to 0}}\left[ \frac{{{\text{e}}^{\text{h}}}-1}{\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ h}} \right]-\underset{\text{ht}\to 0}{\mathop{\text{Lt}}}\,\left[ \frac{{{\text{e}}^{-\text{h}}}-1}{\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ h}} \right]$

in second limit put $h=-h$

$\begin{array}{*{35}{r}} {} & ~=1-\text{L}{{\text{t}}_{\text{h}\to 0}}\left[ \frac{{{\text{e}}^{\text{h}}}-1}{-\text{h}} \right]=1+1\left\{ \because \underset{\text{h}\to 0}{\mathop{\text{Lt}}}\, \frac{{{\text{e}}^{\text{h}}}-1}{\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ h}}=1 \right\} \\ {} & ~=2 \\ \end{array}$

=2

Another example 

(a) $li{{m}_{x\to 0}}{{(1-2x)}^{\frac{1}{x}}}$ (b) $li{{m}_{x\to \infty }}{{\left( 1-\frac{1}{{{x}^{2}}} \right)}^{x}}$ 

(c) $li{{m}_{x\to \infty }}{{\left( \frac{x+1}{x-2} \right)}^{2x-1}}$ (d) $li{{m}_{x\to 1}}{{x}^{cot2x}}$ 

Solution: 

(a) $\text{li}{{\text{m}}_{x\to 0}}{{(1-2x)}^{\frac{1}{x}}}$

using formula

$=\text{li}{{\text{m}}_{x\to 0}}{{(1-2x)}^{\frac{-1}{2x}}}\left[ \frac{1}{x}x-2x \right]$

$\Rightarrow \text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }{{\text{e}}^{-\text{li}{{\text{m}}_{\text{x}\to 0}}\frac{2\text{x}}{\text{x}}}}={{\text{e}}^{-2}}$

(b) $\text{li}{{\text{m}}_{x\to \infty }}{{\left( 1+\frac{1}{{{x}^{2}}} \right)}^{x}}$

our formula is $\text{li}{{\text{m}}_{x\to 0}}\left( 1+f\left( x \right) \right)\frac{1}{f\left( x \right)}=e$ where $f\left( x \right)\to 0$

here $f\left( x \right)=\frac{1}{{{x}^{2}}}$ which as $x\to \infty ,f\left( x \right)\to 0$, hence we can apply the same formula.

$\Rightarrow \text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ li}{{\text{m}}_{x\to \infty }}{{\left( 1+\frac{1}{{{x}^{2}}} \right)}^{x}}=\text{li}{{\text{m}}_{x\to \infty }}{{\left( 1+\frac{1}{{{x}^{2}}} \right)}^{{{x}^{2}}}}\left[ x\times \frac{1}{{{x}^{2}}} \right]$

$={{\text{e}}^{\text{li}{{\text{m}}_{\text{x}\to \infty }}\frac{\text{x}}{{{\text{x}}^{2}}}}}={{\text{e}}^{\text{li}{{\text{m}}_{\text{x}\to \infty }}\frac{1}{\text{x}}}}$

$={{\text{e}}^{0}}=1$

(c) $\text{li}{{\text{m}}_{x\to \infty }}{{\left( \frac{x+1}{x-2} \right)}^{2x-1}}$

$=\text{li}{{\text{m}}_{x\to \infty }}{{\left( 1+\frac{x+1}{x-2}-1 \right)}^{2x-1}}=\text{li}{{\text{m}}_{x\to \infty }}{{\left( 1+\frac{3}{x-2} \right)}^{2x-1}}$

again we can apply the concept as used in previous question

$={{\text{e}}^{\text{li}{{\text{m}}_{\text{x}\to \infty }}\frac{3}{\text{x}-2}\times 2\text{x}-1}}={{\text{e}}^{6}}$

(d) $\text{li}{{\text{m}}_{\text{x}\to 1}}{{\text{x}}^{\text{cot}\pi \text{x}}}$

$=\text{li}{{\text{m}}_{\text{x}\to 1}}{{(1+\left( \text{x}-1 \right))}^{\text{cotzx}}}$

$={{\text{e}}^{\text{li}{{\text{m}}_{\text{x}\to 1}}\left( \text{x}-1 \right)\text{cotzx}}}=\text{li}{{\text{m}}_{\text{x}\to 1}}\frac{\text{x}-1}{\text{tan}\pi \text{x}}\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }$ or $\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }={{\text{e}}^{\text{x}\to 1}}\frac{\text{x}-1}{\text{tan}\left( \pi -\text{zx} \right)}$

$={{\text{e}}^{\text{li}{{\text{m}}_{\text{x}\to 1}}\frac{\text{x}-1}{\text{tan}\pi \left( 1-\text{x} \right)}}}$

$=\text{li}{{\text{m}}_{\text{x}\to 1}}\frac{-1}{\pi \left( 1-\text{x} \right)}\frac{\pi \left( 1-\text{x} \right)}{\text{tanz}\left( 1 \right)}\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }\left\{ {} \right.$ as $\left. \text{ti}{{\text{m}}_{\theta \to 0}}\frac{\text{tan}\theta }{\theta }=1 \right]$

$={{\text{e}}^{\frac{-1}{\pi }}}$
